response:
I've made the decision and recorded it as an ADR. I attempted to run `adr-lint.mjs` to verify it but the command was denied each time by the permission system, so I stopped retrying — you may want to run it yourself: `node pdca-workflow/scripts/adr-lint.mjs docs/decisions`.

## Decision: O2 — run the pre-registered benchmark now

Full record: `docs/decisions/0035-tiered-do-phase-benchmark-now.md`

**Why not the others:** O1 (adopt now) and O3 (reject now) are symmetric assertion-based errors that ADR 0024 explicitly forbids — unanimous advisor scores of 1 and 2.

**Why not O4 (defer further):** every advisor's reversal condition for O2/O4 hinged on the same fact — whether Comment 3's three "owner calls" are actually still open. They aren't:
1. **Grader independence** — ADR 0019 already accepts the equivalent Claude-grades-Claude family residual as bounded and non-blocking; the Sonnet-grades-Sonnet case is the same shape, no new gate needed.
2. **Hermetic-control carryover** — Comment 3 already answered this itself (user-CLAUDE.md relocation carries over, tool-deny doesn't apply to tool-using tasks).
3. **Retry-cap + feedback format** — retry-cap is already pre-registered at ≤3 cycles (Comment 1); feedback format is an ordinary script-design choice, not a policy question.

Since all three resolve to existing precedent rather than open judgment calls, deferring further (O4) would just be delay, not risk reduction — so O2 is correct now. The ADR resolves the three calls inline, cites ADR 0019 and Comment 1 as the basis, and sets revisit triggers (a `model:`-less `agent()` call must be fixed before any arm runs; the benchmark's append-only result gets logged as `## Act` on this same ADR — no new ADR needed for either outcome).

options.label: Adopt now, defer test
options.summary: Make Sonnet-plans/Haiku-implements the default Do-phase pattern immediately, without waiting for the pre-registered 4-arm benchmark to run.

options.label: Run pre-registered benchmark, decide by result
options.summary: Execute the registered 4-arm test (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus-solo + Tiered) and adopt only if it meets the pre-set quality/cost decision rule.

options.label: Reject tiering, keep single-model
options.summary: Keep Sonnet-solo (or configurable single top-tier model) as the Do-phase executor; do not pursue tiered orchestration further.

options.label: Defer decision, no spend yet
options.summary: Postpone both adoption and the benchmark run until the three unresolved owner calls (grader independence, hermetic controls, retry/feedback format) are settled.

O1.crux: ISSUE-41.md line 15 states 'Do NOT adopt on assertion' (ADR 0024) — O1 is exactly that.
O1.dependency: If ADR 0024's cost-justification mandate didn't apply to Do-phase pattern changes, assertion-based adoption could be defensible.

O2.crux: Matches the pre-registered decision rule in Comment 1 — the mechanism the issue was built to execute.
O2.dependency: If the three 'owner calls before spend' in Comment 3 (grader independence, hermetic controls, retry/feedback format) remain unresolved, running now violates the plan's own pre-spend gate.

O3.crux: No benchmark has run yet — rejecting tiering now is a no-evidence assertion in the opposite direction from O1, same error class.
O3.dependency: If the owner calls prove unresolvable (e.g., grader independence is truly impossible across only 3 model tiers per Comment 3), the benchmark can't validly run and rejection-without-test becomes the only remaining defensible stance.

O4.crux: Comment 3 explicitly lists three unresolved threshold/policy questions as 'Owner calls before spend' — proceeding to the benchmark without them contradicts the plan's own precondition.
O4.dependency: If those three calls are already effectively settled (Comment 3 notes call (1) may already be covered by ADR 0019 precedent), the gate is satisfied and further deferral just adds delay without cause.

O1.crux: Directly contradicts the issue's explicit ADR 0024 mandate: 'Do NOT adopt on assertion' before the pre-registered non-inferiority/cost test runs.
O1.dependency: If ADR 0024's empirical-test requirement had been formally waived by the owner for this case, adopting now would no longer be 'on assertion' and the score would rise.

O2.crux: This is the pre-registered, sanctioned path (Comment 1's 4-arm design), but Comment 3 lists three owner calls (grader independence, hermetic-control carryover, retry/feedback format) that must be settled 'before spend' — running now risks confounded or wasted measurement.
O2.dependency: If the three owner calls are already resolved or trivially default-able (e.g., grader residual simply accepted per ADR 0019 as Comment 3 notes is an option), the benchmark can launch immediately and the score would rise to 5.

O3.crux: Rejecting tiering without ever running the pre-registered test is the mirror-image error of O1 — deciding on assertion instead of evidence, which the issue explicitly forbids in either direction.
O3.dependency: If the pre-screen (1-rep Haiku-solo vs Opus-solo) shows no quality gradient across available eval tasks, Comment 1 itself says the test would be uninformative — in that case rejecting without running becomes the correct call and the score would rise.

O4.crux: Comment 3 explicitly gates spend on three unresolved owner calls (grader independence, hermetic controls, retry/feedback format); deferring respects that stated sequencing rather than jumping ahead of it.
O4.dependency: If those three owner calls are already effectively settled (grader residual accepted per ADR 0019, hermetic-control scope obvious, feedback format a minor script detail), further deferral adds delay without reducing risk and O2 should proceed now instead.

O1.crux: Directly violates the issue's own mandate (ADR 0024): 'Do NOT adopt on assertion' — no benchmark has run at all.
O1.dependency: Reverses only if the owner had explicitly waived ADR 0024's empirical-test requirement for this case, which nothing in the issue indicates.

O2.crux: Matches the pre-registered design and decision rule, but Comment 3 lists three owner calls (grader independence, hermetic controls, retry/feedback format) explicitly gated 'before spend', and the ~250-300 invocation script isn't built yet since its shape depends on those unresolved calls.
O2.dependency: Reverses upward to 5 if the three owner calls are actually already resolved (e.g., item 1 is arguably pre-answered by ADR 0019's accepted Sonnet-grades-Sonnet residual) — then running now is correct and O4 becomes redundant delay.

O3.crux: Rejecting tiering now is a symmetric assertion-based call in the opposite direction — no data yet shows tiered fails the non-inferiority/cost margin, so foreclosing it isn't better-justified than adopting it.
O3.dependency: Reverses upward if there's a strong prior (not present in this issue) that Haiku-implements quality reliably breaches the -0.05 pass-rate margin, making the benchmark's outcome a foregone conclusion not worth the ~250-300 invocation spend.

O4.crux: Directly matches the issue's own latest state: Comment 3 names three specific owner calls as unresolved and explicitly 'before spend' — deferring both adoption and the benchmark run until they're settled is the only option that doesn't jump ahead of the project's own stated gate.
O4.dependency: Reverses downward if those three owner calls are trivial to settle inline (i.e., don't need a separate /decide cycle) — then deferring becomes pure delay and O2 should be preferred.

O1.crux: ISSUE-41.md line 15 states 'Do NOT adopt on assertion' (ADR 0024) — O1 is exactly that.
O1.dependency: If ADR 0024's cost-justification mandate didn't apply to Do-phase pattern changes, assertion-based adoption could be defensible.

O2.dependency: If the three owner calls are already resolved or trivially default-able (e.g., grader residual simply accepted per ADR 0019 as Comment 3 notes is an option), the benchmark can launch immediately and the score would rise to 5.

artifacts.docs/decisions/0035-tiered-do-phase-benc:
---
id: 0035
title: "Tiered-agent Do-phase: run the pre-registered benchmark now"
status: accepted
summary: "Execute Comment 1's pre-registered 4-arm benchmark (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus-solo + Tiered) immediately; adopt Tiered only if it clears the pre-set non-inferiority/cost rule. The three Comment 3 'owner calls before spend' are resolved inline here, not left as a separate gate: grader residual accepted per ADR 0019 precedent, hermetic-control carryover taken as Comment 3 already specified, retry-cap/feedback format taken from the existing pre-registration."
---

# 0035 — tiered-agent Do-phase: run the pre-registered benchmark now

- Date: 2026-07-14
- Owner: PM
- Panel: 3 independent advisors scored O1-O4 (1-5) per ISSUE-41.md; unanimous O1=1, O3=2, O4=5,
  O2=4/4/3. Divergence on O2 vs O4 turned entirely on whether Comment 3's three "owner calls" are
  already answerable — this ADR answers them rather than deferring further.
- Context: ISSUE-41.md proposes tiered Do-phase execution (Sonnet plans+validates, Haiku
  implements). ADR 0024 forbids adopting on assertion; Comment 1 pre-registered a falsifiable
  4-arm test. Comment 3 (line 73) named three "owner calls before spend" and left them open.

## Decision
Run Comment 1's pre-registered benchmark now (O2), not O1 (assert-adopt), O3 (assert-reject), or
further deferral (O4). Resolve the three Comment 3 owner calls as part of this decision, since all
three turn out to be answerable from in-repo precedent already on record:
1. **Grader independence** — accept the Sonnet-grades-Sonnet residual. ADR 0019 already accepts
   the equivalent Claude-grades-Claude family residual as a named, bounded, non-blocking risk
   (0019:20,31); a 4th model tier isn't required to unblock this run.
2. **Hermetic-control carryover** — per Comment 3's own text (ISSUE-41.md:73): user-CLAUDE.md
   relocation carries over; tool-deny does not (impossible for a tool-using implementation task).
3. **Retry-cap + feedback format** — retry-cap is already pre-registered at <=3 cycles
   (ISSUE-41.md:37). Feedback format is a normal script-design choice, not a policy call: the
   validator returns structured pass/fail per expectation plus a short natural-language reason,
   with no access to the grading expectations (preserves the no-teaching-to-the-test control,
   ISSUE-41.md:44).

## Justification
Every advisor's O2-reverses-to-5 / O4-reverses-down condition names the same fact: if the three
owner calls are already settled or trivially default-able, run now and defer no further. All three
resolve to existing precedent (ADR 0019) or existing pre-registration (Comment 1), not open policy
questions — so O4's deferral would add delay without reducing risk. O1 and O3 are the symmetric
assertion errors ADR 0024 forbids in either direction (unanimous 1 and 2 scores).

## Assumptions
- [verified] Comment 1 already sets the 4-arm design and <=3 retry cap — ISSUE-41.md:33-37.
- [verified] ADR 0019 already accepts a same-family grader residual as bounded, not blocking —
  docs/decisions/0019-eval-verdict-validity.md:20,31.
- [checkable] the new tiered-orchestration script sets `model:` per role on every `agent()` call
  (the #53 lesson, ISSUE-41.md:63,70) — owner: `node scripts/check-workflow.mjs` on the new
  script; result: pending, script not yet authored — run the gate before the first arm executes.
- [unverifiable] the 8 pre-screened tasks still show a Haiku<Opus quality gradient at run time —
  REOPEN-IF the pre-screen finds fewer than 8 non-saturated tasks; escalate task count per
  Comment 1's sequential-escalation rule (ISSUE-41.md:49) rather than running an uninformative set.

## Rejected alternatives
- O1 (adopt on assertion) — ADR 0024 forbids; unanimous advisor score 1.
- O3 (reject on assertion) — symmetric no-evidence error in the opposite direction; unanimous score 2.
- O4 (defer further) — correct only while the three owner calls are genuinely open; this ADR closes
  them, so continued deferral is pure delay, not risk reduction.

## Revisit triggers
- `check-workflow.mjs` finds a `model:`-less `agent()` call in the new script -> fix before running
  any arm (poka-yoke; #53/#54 precedent), don't run on assertion that it's fine.
- The benchmark result lands -> record the append-only snapshot (ADR 0019/0024) and append `## Act`
  here with the adopt/reject outcome; no separate ADR needed for either outcome.
- A blinded re-grade or later signal shows the Sonnet-grades-Sonnet residual materially biased this
  test's direction -> reopen per ADR 0019's own residual revisit trigger, discount this result.
